description: Frozen CertOps route namespaces and stable routes. The controller lane added only narrow cert-manager observation, provisioning intent, cluster-bound command, and just-in-time mutation-authorization surfaces. The agent protocol is implemented server-side: the four /api/v1/certops/agent routes are now live control-plane endpoints (register, heartbeat, claim, results) with Ed25519-signed job dispatch, single-use result nonces, and claim-ownership re-proof.

notes: Human/dashboard surface. Inherits global session + workspace membership + frozen-workspace guards.
Subject to CSRF protection like other workspace mutations.
The workspace kill-switch settings GET and PUT routes require a human cookie/session user; internal worker bearer credentials are not accepted.

POST /jobs/bulk-renew queues renewal jobs for up to 100 managed certificates in one request through the same manual job-creation path as POST /jobs (manager role, kill switch, approval gate, payload validation). It responds with a partial-failure envelope: per-certificate successes and failures are reported item by item and never abort the batch.
Machine surface for executor writes plus narrow controller observation and provisioning transport. Scoped API-token bearer auth only; machine routes never use cookies.

Allowed scopes are certops:read, certops:events:write, certops:jobs:read, certops:evidence:write, certops:observations:write, and certops:provision:execute. certops:read implies certops:jobs:read only, never a write scope; neither controller scope is implied by another scope and either requires an immutable controllerClusterId binding. Empty required-scope configuration is invalid.

CSRF-exempt only for the exact planned POST machine routes (aggregate executor events, controller observations, provisioning command fetch and mutation authorization, and the two per-job aliases), including Express-compatible case and one trailing slash. Nearby paths remain CSRF-protected.
Ingest payloads are scanned and rejected (422) if they contain private key material.

POST /api/v1/certops/executor/events is the aggregate executor ingestion route. POST /api/v1/certops/jobs/{jobId}/events and POST /api/v1/certops/jobs/{jobId}/evidence are stable path-scoped executor machine routes.

The three executor routes retain the same idempotency, redaction, private-key rejection, dedicated pre-parser boundary, rate-limit, and audit behavior. POST /api/v1/certops/executor/observations is passive controller-specific transport. POST /api/v1/certops/executor/provisioning-commands/next is a cluster-bound provision-command transport that redelivers only server-owned controller_provisioning jobs safely. POST /api/v1/certops/executor/provisioning-commands/{jobId}/authorize-mutation is a narrow just-in-time rollout/workspace/job recheck immediately before each Certificate create or patch and every mutation retry. Neither provisioning route introduces agent claims, heartbeats, signatures, nonces, leases, or polling protocol. Both reject private material before scope or extension gates and deny mutation work while a workspace is paused.
Outbound-only agent polling surface. The control plane never connects to agents.
Enrollment and steady state use different credentials, by design.
POST /agent/register uses agentBootstrapTokenAuth: a single-use, hashed, expiring bootstrap token an admin creates. Registration returns a per-agent credential, shown once.
/agent/heartbeat, /agent/jobs/claim, /agent/jobs/results use agentCredentialAuth: the issued per-agent credential (bearer). Bootstrap tokens do not authorize steady-state calls.

All routes CSRF-exempt (token-authenticated, non-cookie). Namespace frozen in Phase 0; request/response bodies are now specified authoritatively by packages/contracts/certops/agent-protocol.schema.json (message envelope, additionalProperties false). POST /agent/jobs/results accepts both result and evidence messageTypes; the server dispatches on the envelope messageType.

privateKeyMaterial.notes: Any request body containing private key material is rejected at the boundary. The control plane never stores, generates, exports, transmits, or processes private key material.

workspaceCertOpsPaused.notes: The workspace-local kill switch blocks new CertOps jobs and future dispatch/provision side effects only. It does not block inventory, status, audit, evidence, or executor event reporting for existing work. It composes with, and never replaces, the deployment-wide certops.enabled rollout flag.
